description_long: This object is a traditional wooden hand plane, commonly used in woodworking for smoothing or shaping wood surfaces. The body is made of light-colored wood with a rectangular block shape, featuring a prominent, curved black handle at the rear for gripping. At the front, a metal blade is set at an angle, held in place by a wooden wedge, and protrudes slightly from the bottom for shaving wood. The sides of the body display some stamped markings and text, adding to its utilitarian appearance. The overall finish is matte, with visible wood grain and some signs of wear, indicating practical use. The handle and blade components contrast visually with the lighter wood, making the functional parts easily distinguishable.
description: A traditional wooden hand plane with a light wood body, black curved handle, and angled metal blade. The tool features stamped markings and a matte, worn finish.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the wooden body, the black handle, angled metal blade, and stamped markings.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the opening for the blade and the end of the handle, with the blade edge partially visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handle and blade from the other side, with less visible text.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the curved black handle and the back of the wooden body.
attribute_brand_logo: There is a stamped marking with text and symbols on the side of the wooden body, featuring a serial number and some characters, but no recognizable brand logo.
attribute_handle_design: The handle is curved, made of black material (likely plastic or painted wood), with a smooth surface and ergonomic contour for gripping. It is attached at the rear of the body and shaped for comfortable hand placement.
attribute_staple_exit_slot: Not visible; this object does not have a staple exit slot as it is a hand plane, not a stapler.
